revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=金峰神社 |画像= |所在地=[[岐阜県]][[美濃市]]片知猿洞 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |祭神=蔵王権現 |社格= |創建=[[天暦]]年間 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }}
'''金峰神社'''（きんぶじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[美濃市]]片知にある[[神社]]である。[[高賀山]]を囲む'''高賀六社'''の1社で、[[板取川]]（[[長良川]]の支流）の支流の片知川沿いにある。
== 祭神 == *[[蔵王権現]] **実際の御神体は、[[阿弥陀如来]]、[[十一面観世音菩薩]]、[[地蔵菩薩]]であるという。また、3体の仏像の脇には[[金剛蔵王]]と[[天部]]が祀られている。
== 由緒 == 社伝によると、[[天暦]]年間（10世紀中頃）、この地に[[妖怪]][[さるとらへび]]が住み付き、村人に危害を加えているのを聞いた[[朝廷]]が、[[藤原高光]]をこの地に遣わした<ref>[[承平 (日本)|承平]]3年（[[933年]]）の説もあるが、藤原高光の生誕年との矛盾がある。</ref>。高光が最初にさるとらへびを見つけたのがこの地という。このことから地名を「形知」（現・片知）と名づけ、形知の社として創建したのが始まりという。後に高賀山一帯は、美濃国の霊山とされる。[[修験道]]の影響を受け、[[江戸時代]]以前に蔵王権現社に改称する。[[明治時代]]初期、金峰神社に改称する。これは[[奈良県]]の[[金峯神社 (吉野町)|金峯神社]]に由来するという。
== 高賀六社 == 高賀山を主峰とした山（瓢ケ岳、今渕ケ岳、片知山など）の山麓にあり、高賀山を囲む6つの神社。かつては'''六社めぐり'''という、この6社を尾根伝いに1日で歩いて巡る[[苦行]]が存在した。 * [[高賀神社]]（岐阜県[[関市]]） * [[那比新宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[本宮神社 (郡上市)|本宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[星宮神社 (郡上市)|星宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[瀧神社]]（岐阜県[[美濃市]]） * 金峰神社（岐阜県美濃市）
== 交通機関 == * [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]牧谷線「金門橋」バス停下車。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃市]]

{{神社 |名称=金峰神社 |画像= |所在地=[[岐阜県]][[美濃市]]片知猿洞 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |祭神=蔵王権現 |社格= |創建=[[天暦]]年間 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }}
'''金峰神社'''（きんぶじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[美濃市]]片知にある[[神社]]である。[[高賀山]]を囲む'''高賀六社'''の1社で、[[板取川]]（[[長良川]]の支流）の支流の片知川沿いにある。
== 祭神 == *[[蔵王権現]] **実際の御神体は、[[阿弥陀如来]]、[[十一面観世音菩薩]]、[[地蔵菩薩]]であるという。また、3体の仏像の脇には[[金剛蔵王]]と[[天部]]が祀られている。
== 由緒 == 社伝によると、[[天暦]]年間（10世紀中頃）、この地に[[妖怪]][[さるとらへび]]が住み付き、村人に危害を加えているのを聞いた[[朝廷]]が、[[藤原高光]]をこの地に遣わした<ref>[[承平 (日本)|承平]]3年（[[933年]]）の説もあるが、藤原高光の生誕年との矛盾がある。</ref>。高光が最初にさるとらへびを見つけたのがこの地という。このことから地名を「形知」（現・片知）と名づけ、形知の社として創建したのが始まりという。後に高賀山一帯は、美濃国の霊山とされる。[[修験道]]の影響を受け、[[江戸時代]]以前に蔵王権現社に改称する。[[明治時代]]初期、金峰神社に改称する。これは[[奈良県]]の[[金峯神社 (吉野町)|金峯神社]]に由来するという。
== 高賀六社 == 高賀山を主峰とした山（瓢ケ岳、今渕ケ岳、片知山など）の山麓にあり、高賀山を囲む6つの神社。かつては'''六社めぐり'''という、この6社を尾根伝いに1日で歩いて巡る[[苦行]]が存在した。 * [[高賀神社]]（岐阜県[[関市]]） * [[那比新宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[本宮神社 (郡上市)|本宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[星宮神社 (郡上市)|星宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[瀧神社]]（岐阜県[[美濃市]]） * 金峰神社（岐阜県美濃市）
== 交通機関 == * [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]牧谷線「金門橋」バス停下車。

revision.comment: 地元の集落からの話を追加
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=金峰神社 |画像=[[Image: Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |300px]] |所在地=[[岐阜県]][[美濃市]]片知猿洞 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |祭神=蔵王権現 |社格= |創建=[[天暦]]年間 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }}

== 祭神 == *[[蔵王権現]] **実際の御神体は、[[阿弥陀如来]]、[[十一面観世音菩薩]]、[[地蔵菩薩]]であるという。また、3体の仏像の脇には[[金剛蔵王]]と[[天部]]が祀られている。
== 由緒 == [[ファイル:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[岐阜県]][[関市]]の[[高賀神社]]にある「高光公とさるとらへび」の像]] 社伝によると、[[天暦]]年間（10世紀中頃）、この地に[[妖怪]][[さるとらへび]]が住み付き、村人に危害を加えているのを聞いた[[朝廷]]が、[[藤原高光]]をこの地に遣わした<ref>[[承平 (日本)|承平]]3年（[[933年]]）の説もあるが、藤原高光の生誕年との矛盾がある。</ref>。高光が最初にさるとらへびを見つけたのがこの地とされる。このことから地名を「形知」（現・片知）と名づけ、形知の社として創建したのが始まりと伝わる。後に高賀山一帯は、美濃国の霊山として崇められた。[[修験道]]の影響を受け、[[江戸時代]]以前に蔵王権現社に改称する。[[明治時代]]初期、金峰神社に改称する。これは[[奈良県]]の[[金峯神社 (吉野町)|金峯神社]]に由来すると伝わる。

== 高賀山六社 == 高賀山を主峰とした山（瓢ケ岳、今渕ケ岳、片知山など）の山麓にあり、高賀山を囲む6つの神社。かつては'''六社めぐり'''という、この6社を尾根伝いに1日で歩いて巡る[[苦行]]が存在した。 * [[高賀神社]]（岐阜県[[関市]]） * [[那比新宮神社]]（岐阜県[[郡上市]]） * [[本宮神社 (郡上市)|那比本宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[星宮神社 (郡上市)|星宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[瀧神社]]（岐阜県[[美濃市]]） * 金峰神社（岐阜県美濃市）
== 交通機関 == * [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]：牧谷線「金門橋」バス停下車。

'''金峰神社'''（かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481 |title=金峰神社詳細 - 岐阜県神社庁 |access-date=2022年10月30日}}</ref>）は、[[岐阜県]][[美濃市]]片知にある[[神社]]である。

なお周辺集落では金峰神社（かねみねじんじゃ）と呼ばれる。

== 由緒 == 社伝によると、[[天暦]]年間（10世紀中頃）、この地に[[妖怪]][[さるとらへび]]が住み付き、村人に危害を加えているのを聞いた[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]が、[[藤原高光]]をこの地に遣わした<ref>[[承平 (日本)|承平]]3年（[[933年]]）の説もあるが、藤原高光の生誕年との矛盾がある。</ref>。高光が最初にさるとらへびを見つけたのがこの地とされる。このことから地名を「形知」（現在の片知）と名づけ、形知の社として創建したのが始まりと伝わる。後に高賀山一帯は、美濃国の霊山として崇められた。[[修験道]]の影響を受け、[[江戸時代]]以前に蔵王権現社に改称する。[[明治時代]]初期、金峰神社に改称する。これは[[奈良県]]の[[金峯神社 (吉野町)|金峯神社]]に由来すると伝わる。
== 高賀山六社 == 高賀山を主峰とした山（瓢ケ岳、今渕ケ岳、片知山など）の山麓にあり、高賀山を囲む6つの神社。かつては'''六社めぐり'''という、この6社を尾根伝いに1日で歩いて巡る[[苦行]]が存在した。 * [[高賀神社]]（岐阜県[[関市]]） * [[那比新宮神社]]（岐阜県[[郡上市]]） * [[本宮神社 (郡上市)|那比本宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[星宮神社 (郡上市)|星宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[瀧神社]]（岐阜県[[美濃市]]） * 金峰神社（岐阜県美濃市）
== 交通機関 == * [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]：牧谷線「金門橋」バス停下車。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ 岐阜県神社庁公式ホームページ] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:美濃市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃市の建築物]]

[[ファイル:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|[[岐阜県]][[関市]]の[[高賀神社]]にある「高光公とさるとらへび」の像]] [[ファイル:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|山麓から望む高賀神社の[[鳥居]]と[[高賀山]]]]
'''金峰神社'''（かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481 |title=金峰神社詳細 - 岐阜県神社庁 |accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>）は、[[岐阜県]][[美濃市]]片知にある[[神社]]。『'''高賀山信仰'''』における中心的な社(高賀山六社)の一社である。
== 概要 == [[高賀山]]を囲む'''高賀山六社'''の1社で、[[板取川]]（[[長良川]]の支流）の支流の片知川沿いにある。
なお周辺集落では金峰神社（かねみねじんじゃ）と呼ばれる。
== 祭神 == * [[蔵王権現]]
※ 実際の御神体は、[[阿弥陀如来]]、[[十一面観世音菩薩]]、[[地蔵菩薩]]であるという。また、3体の仏像の脇には[[金剛蔵王]]と[[天部]]が祀られている。

== 高賀山六社 == 高賀山を主峰とした山（瓢ケ岳、今渕ケ岳、片知山など）の山麓にあり、高賀山を囲む6つの神社。かつては'''六社めぐり'''という、この6社を尾根伝いに1日で歩いて巡る[[苦行]]が存在した。 * [[高賀神社]]（岐阜県[[関市]]） * [[那比新宮神社]]（岐阜県[[郡上市]]） * [[本宮神社 (郡上市)|那比本宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[星宮神社 (郡上市)|星宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[瀧神社]]（岐阜県[[美濃市]]） * 金峰神社（岐阜県美濃市）
== 交通機関 == * [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]：牧谷線「金門橋」バス停下車。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ 岐阜県神社庁公式ホームページ] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:美濃市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃市の建築物]]

== 由緒 == 社伝によると、[[天暦]]年間（10世紀中頃）、この地に[[妖怪]][[さるとらへび]]が住み付き、村人に危害を加えているのを聞いた[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]が、[[藤原高光]]をこの地に遣わした<ref>[[承平 (日本)|承平]]3年（[[933年]]）の説もあるが、藤原高光の生誕年との矛盾がある。</ref>。高光が最初にさるとらへびを見つけたのがこの地とされる。このことから地名を「形知」（現在の片知）と名づけ、形知の社として創建したのが始まりと伝わる。後に高賀山一帯は、美濃国の霊山として崇められた。[[修験道]]の影響を受け、[[江戸時代]]以前に蔵王権現社に改称する。[[明治時代]]初期、金峰神社に改称する。これは[[奈良県]]の[[金峯神社 (吉野町)|金峯神社]]に由来すると伝わる。
== 高賀山六社 == 高賀山を主峰とした山（瓢ケ岳、今渕ケ岳、片知山など）の山麓にあり、高賀山を囲む6つの神社。かつては'''六社めぐり'''という、この6社を尾根伝いに1日で歩いて巡る[[苦行]]が存在した。 * [[高賀神社]]（岐阜県[[関市]]） * [[那比新宮神社]]（岐阜県[[郡上市]]） * [[本宮神社 (郡上市)|那比本宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[星宮神社 (郡上市)|星宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[瀧神社]]（岐阜県[[美濃市]]） * 金峰神社（岐阜県美濃市）
== 交通機関 == * [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]：牧谷線「金門橋」バス停下車。

なお周辺集落では金峰神社（かねみねじんじゃ）と呼ばれる。
== 祭神 == * [[蔵王権現]]
※ 実際の御神体は、[[阿弥陀如来]]、[[十一面観世音菩薩]]、[[地蔵菩薩]]であるという。また、3体の仏像の脇には[[金剛蔵王]]と[[天部]]が祀られている。
== 由緒 == 社伝によると、[[天暦]]年間（10世紀中頃）、この地に[[妖怪]][[さるとらへび]]が住み付き、村人に危害を加えているのを聞いた[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]が、[[藤原高光]]をこの地に遣わした<ref>[[承平 (日本)|承平]]3年（[[933年]]）の説もあるが、藤原高光の生誕年との矛盾がある。</ref>。高光が最初にさるとらへびを見つけたのがこの地とされる。このことから地名を「形知」（現在の片知）と名づけ、形知の社として創建したのが始まりと伝わる。後に高賀山一帯は、美濃国の霊山として崇められた。[[修験道]]の影響を受け、[[江戸時代]]以前に蔵王権現社に改称する。[[明治時代]]初期、金峰神社に改称する。これは[[奈良県]]の[[金峯神社 (吉野町)|金峯神社]]に由来すると伝わる。
== 高賀山六社 == 高賀山を主峰とした山（瓢ケ岳、今渕ケ岳、片知山など）の山麓にあり、高賀山を囲む6つの神社。かつては'''六社めぐり'''という、この6社を尾根伝いに1日で歩いて巡る[[苦行]]が存在した。 * [[高賀神社]]（岐阜県[[関市]]） * [[那比新宮神社]]（岐阜県[[郡上市]]） * [[本宮神社 (郡上市)|那比本宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[星宮神社 (郡上市)|星宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[瀧神社]]（岐阜県[[美濃市]]） * 金峰神社（岐阜県美濃市）
== 交通機関 == * [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]：牧谷線「金門橋」バス停下車。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}

なお周辺集落では金峰神社（かねみねじんじゃ）と呼ばれる。

{{神社 |名称= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社}} | image=[[File: Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |250px]] |所在地= {{ill|Mino, Gifu|ja|美濃市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |祭神= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} |創建= {{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} period (947–957) }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社}}, {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Mino, Gifu|ja|美濃市}}, Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Itadori River|ja|板取川}}, itself a tributary of the {{ill|Nagara River|ja|長良川}}.
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|ja|地蔵菩薩}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王}} and {{ill|Tenbu|ja|天部}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|ja|藤原高光}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kinpusen-ji|ja|金峯山寺}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|ja|吉野町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社}} ({{ill|Gujo, Gifu|ja|郡上市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

{{神社 |名称= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社}} | image=[[File: Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |250px]] |所在地= {{ill|Mino, Gifu|ja|美濃市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |deity= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} |創建= {{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} period (947–957) }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社}}, {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Mino, Gifu|ja|美濃市}}, Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Itadori River|ja|板取川}}, itself a tributary of the {{ill|Nagara River|ja|長良川}}.
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|ja|地蔵菩薩}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王}} and {{ill|Tenbu|ja|天部}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|ja|藤原高光}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kinpusen-ji|ja|金峯山寺}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|ja|吉野町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社}} ({{ill|Gujo, Gifu|ja|郡上市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kinpusen-ji|ja|金峯山寺}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|ja|吉野町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社}} ({{ill|Gujo, Gifu|ja|郡上市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

{{infobox religious building |name = Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)|native_name= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社}} | image=[[File: Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |250px]] |address= {{ill|Mino, Gifu|ja|美濃市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |deity= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} |創建= {{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} period (947–957) }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社}}, {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Mino, Gifu|ja|美濃市}}, Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Itadori River|ja|板取川}}, itself a tributary of the {{ill|Nagara River|ja|長良川}}.
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|ja|地蔵菩薩}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王}} and {{ill|Tenbu|ja|天部}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|ja|藤原高光}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kinpusen-ji|ja|金峯山寺}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|ja|吉野町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社}} ({{ill|Gujo, Gifu|ja|郡上市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.

== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

{{infobox religious building |name = Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)|native_name= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社}} | image=[[File: Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |250px]] |address= {{ill|Mino, Gifu|ja|美濃市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |deity= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} |創建= {{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} period (947–957) }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社}}, {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Mino, Gifu|ja|美濃市}}, Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Itadori River|ja|板取川}}, itself a tributary of the {{ill|Nagara River|ja|長良川}}.
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|ja|地蔵菩薩}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王}} and {{ill|Tenbu|ja|天部}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|ja|藤原高光}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kinpusen-ji|ja|金峯山寺}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|ja|吉野町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社}} ({{ill|Gujo, Gifu|ja|郡上市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name = Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)|native_name= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社}} | image=[[File: Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |250px]] |address= {{ill|Mino, Gifu|ja|美濃市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |deity= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} |創建= {{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} period (947–957) }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社}}, {{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Mino, Gifu|ja|美濃市}}, Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Itadori River|ja|板取川}}, itself a tributary of the {{ill|Nagara River|ja|長良川}}.
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|ja|地蔵菩薩}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王}} and {{ill|Tenbu|ja|天部}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|ja|藤原高光}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kinpusen-ji|ja|金峯山寺}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|ja|吉野町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社}} ({{ill|Gujo, Gifu|ja|郡上市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake shrines]] [[Category:History of Mino, Gifu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Mino, Gifu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)}}

'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Mino, Gifu|ja|美濃市}}, Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Itadori River|ja|板取川}}, itself a tributary of the {{ill|Nagara River|ja|長良川}}.
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|ja|地蔵菩薩}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王}} and {{ill|Tenbu|ja|天部}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Imperial Court (Japan)|ja|朝廷}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|ja|藤原高光}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kinpusen-ji|ja|金峯山寺}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|ja|吉野町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|ja|関市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社}} ({{ill|Gujo, Gifu|ja|郡上市}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜乗合自動車}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=金峰神社 |画像=[[Image: Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |250px]] |所在地=[[岐阜県]][[美濃市]]片知猿洞 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |祭神=蔵王権現 |社格=村社、[[金幣社|銀幣社]] |創建=[[天暦]]年間 |本殿= |例祭= |神事= }}
[[ファイル:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|[[岐阜県]][[関市]]の[[高賀神社]]にある「高光公とさるとらへび」の像]] [[ファイル:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|山麓から望む高賀神社の[[鳥居]]と[[高賀山]]]]
'''金峰神社'''（かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481 |title=金峰神社詳細 - 岐阜県神社庁 |accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>）は、[[岐阜県]][[美濃市]]片知にある[[神社]]。『'''高賀山信仰'''』における中心的な社(高賀山六社)の一社である。
== 概要 == [[高賀山]]を囲む'''高賀山六社'''の1社で、[[板取川]]（[[長良川]]の支流）の支流の片知川沿いにある。
なお周辺集落では金峰神社（かねみねじんじゃ）と呼ばれる。
== 祭神 == * [[蔵王権現]]
※ 実際の御神体は、[[阿弥陀如来]]、[[十一面観世音菩薩]]、[[地蔵菩薩]]であるという。また、3体の仏像の脇には[[金剛蔵王]]と[[天部]]が祀られている。
== 由緒 == 社伝によると、[[天暦]]年間（10世紀中頃）、この地に[[妖怪]][[さるとらへび]]が住み付き、村人に危害を加えているのを聞いた[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]が、[[藤原高光]]をこの地に遣わした<ref>[[承平 (日本)|承平]]3年（[[933年]]）の説もあるが、藤原高光の生誕年との矛盾がある。</ref>。高光が最初にさるとらへびを見つけたのがこの地とされる。このことから地名を「形知」（現在の片知）と名づけ、形知の社として創建したのが始まりと伝わる。後に高賀山一帯は、美濃国の霊山として崇められた。[[修験道]]の影響を受け、[[江戸時代]]以前に蔵王権現社に改称する。[[明治時代]]初期、金峰神社に改称する。これは[[奈良県]]の[[金峯神社 (吉野町)|金峯神社]]に由来すると伝わる。
== 高賀山六社 == 高賀山を主峰とした山（瓢ケ岳、今渕ケ岳、片知山など）の山麓にあり、高賀山を囲む6つの神社。かつては'''六社めぐり'''という、この6社を尾根伝いに1日で歩いて巡る[[苦行]]が存在した。 * [[高賀神社]]（岐阜県[[関市]]） * [[那比新宮神社]]（岐阜県[[郡上市]]） * [[本宮神社 (郡上市)|那比本宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[星宮神社 (郡上市)|星宮神社]]（岐阜県郡上市） * [[瀧神社]]（岐阜県[[美濃市]]） * 金峰神社（岐阜県美濃市）
== 交通機関 == * [[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]]：牧谷線「金門橋」バス停下車。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ 岐阜県神社庁公式ホームページ] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:美濃市の歴史]] [[Category:美濃市の建築物]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11647036]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11647036]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)|native_name= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | image=File:Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |address= [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |deity= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} |創建= [[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku era]] period (947–957) |prefecture= {{ill|Mino|qid=Q853835}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.5973417|N|136.9111917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|qq=|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}}, [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|qq=|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135540766}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katachi River|qq=|1=Katachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18701005}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Itadori River|Itadori River]], itself a tributary of the [[:en:Nagara River|Nagara River]].
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]]
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|Tenbu]] deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] dispatched [[:en:Fujiwara no Takamitsu|Fujiwara no Takamitsu]] to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|qq=|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Influenced by [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to [[:en:Kimpusen-ji|Kinpusen-ji]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|qq=|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ([[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|qq=|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ([[:en:Gujō, Gifu|Gujo, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|qq=|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11647036]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11647036]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)|native_name= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | image=File:Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |address= [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |deity= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} |創建= [[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku era]] period (947–957) |prefecture= {{ill|Mino|WD=Q853835}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.5973417|N|136.9111917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|qq=|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}}, [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|qq=|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540766}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katachi River|qq=|1=Katachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18701005}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Itadori River|Itadori River]], itself a tributary of the [[:en:Nagara River|Nagara River]].
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]]
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|Tenbu]] deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] dispatched [[:en:Fujiwara no Takamitsu|Fujiwara no Takamitsu]] to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|qq=|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Influenced by [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to [[:en:Kimpusen-ji|Kinpusen-ji]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|qq=|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ([[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|qq=|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ([[:en:Gujō, Gifu|Gujo, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|qq=|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.

'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540766}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katachi River|1=Katachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18701005}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Itadori River|Itadori River]], itself a tributary of the [[:en:Nagara River|Nagara River]].
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]]
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|Tenbu]] deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] dispatched [[:en:Fujiwara no Takamitsu|Fujiwara no Takamitsu]] to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Influenced by [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to [[:en:Kimpusen-ji|Kinpusen-ji]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ([[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ([[:en:Gujō, Gifu|Gujo, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647036]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647036]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)|native_name= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | image=File:Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |address= [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |deity= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} |創建= [[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku era]] period (947–957) |prefecture= {{ill|Mino|WD=Q853835}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.5973417|N|136.9111917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}}, [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540766}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katachi River|1=Katachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18701005}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Itadori River|Itadori River]], itself a tributary of the [[:en:Nagara River|Nagara River]].
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]]
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|Tenbu]] deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] dispatched [[:en:Fujiwara no Takamitsu|Fujiwara no Takamitsu]] to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Influenced by [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to [[:en:Kimpusen-ji|Kinpusen-ji]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ([[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ([[:en:Gujō, Gifu|Gujo, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ([[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ([[:en:Gujō, Gifu|Gujo, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647036]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647036]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)|native_name= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | image=File:Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |address= [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |deity= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} |創建= [[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku era]] period (947–957) |prefecture= {{ill|Mino|WD=Q853835}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.5973417|N|136.9111917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}}, [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540766}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katachi River|1=Katachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18701005}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Itadori River|Itadori River]], itself a tributary of the [[:en:Nagara River|Nagara River]].
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]]
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|Tenbu]] deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] dispatched [[:en:Fujiwara no Takamitsu|Fujiwara no Takamitsu]] to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Influenced by [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to [[:en:Kimpusen-ji|Kinpusen-ji]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ([[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ([[:en:Gujō, Gifu|Gujo, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.

== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]]
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|Tenbu]] deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] dispatched [[:en:Fujiwara no Takamitsu|Fujiwara no Takamitsu]] to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Influenced by [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to [[:en:Kimpusen-ji|Kinpusen-ji]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ([[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ([[:en:Gujō, Gifu|Gujo, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647036]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647036]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)|native_name= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | image=File:Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |address= [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |deity= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} |創建= [[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku era]] period (947–957) |prefecture= {{ill|Mino|WD=Q853835}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.5973417|N|136.9111917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}}, [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540766}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katachi River|1=Katachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18701005}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Itadori River|Itadori River]], itself a tributary of the [[:en:Nagara River|Nagara River]].
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]]
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|Tenbu]] deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] dispatched [[:en:Fujiwara no Takamitsu|Fujiwara no Takamitsu]] to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Influenced by [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to [[:en:Kimpusen-ji|Kinpusen-ji]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ([[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ([[:en:Gujō, Gifu|Gujo, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ([[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ([[:en:Gujō, Gifu|Gujo, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)
