<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647036]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647036]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name = Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)|native_name= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | image=File:Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |address= [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |deity= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} |創建= [[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku era]] period (947–957) |prefecture= {{ill|Mino|WD=Q853835}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.5973417|N|136.9111917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}}, [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino, Gifu]], Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540766}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katachi River|1=Katachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18701005}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Itadori River|Itadori River]], itself a tributary of the [[:en:Nagara River|Nagara River]].
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]]
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|Tenbu]] deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court (Japan)]] dispatched [[:en:Fujiwara no Takamitsu|Fujiwara no Takamitsu]] to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Influenced by [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to [[:en:Kimpusen-ji|Kinpusen-ji]] in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ([[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ([[:en:Gujō, Gifu|Gujo, Gifu]]) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** [[:en:Gifu Bus|Gifu Bus]] **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Mino, Gifu|en|Mino, Gifu|WD=Q853835|lt=Mino, Gifu}}, Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540766}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katachi River|1=Katachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18701005}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Itadori River|en|Itadori River|WD=Q6092348|lt=Itadori River}}, itself a tributary of the {{ill|Nagara River|en|Nagara River|WD=Q1194094|lt=Nagara River}}.
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amitābha}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Kṣitigarbha}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=Tenbu}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|en|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|WD=Q3071886|lt=Fujiwara no Takamitsu}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kimpusen-ji|en|Kimpusen-ji|WD=Q1741668|lt=Kinpusen-ji}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|en|Yoshino, Nara|WD=Q1203731|lt=Yoshino, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ({{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|WD=Q857243|lt=Gujo, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=Tenbu}} deities.

The {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|en|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|WD=Q3071886|lt=Fujiwara no Takamitsu}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>

Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}. During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kimpusen-ji|en|Kimpusen-ji|WD=Q1741668|lt=Kinpusen-ji}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|en|Yoshino, Nara|WD=Q1203731|lt=Yoshino, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.

Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ({{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|WD=Q857243|lt=Gujo, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.

== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ({{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|WD=Q857243|lt=Gujo, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]]
{{infobox religious building |name = Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)|native_name= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} | image=File:Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |address= {{ill|Mino, Gifu|en|Mino, Gifu|WD=Q853835|lt=Mino, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |deity= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} |創建= {{ill|Tenryaku|en|Tenryaku|WD=Q1346281|lt=Tenryaku era}} period (947–957) |prefecture= {{ill|Mino|WD=Q853835}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.5973417|N|136.9111917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}}, {{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki, Gifu}}]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Mino, Gifu|en|Mino, Gifu|WD=Q853835|lt=Mino, Gifu}}, Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540766}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katachi River|1=Katachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18701005}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Itadori River|en|Itadori River|WD=Q6092348|lt=Itadori River}}, itself a tributary of the {{ill|Nagara River|en|Nagara River|WD=Q1194094|lt=Nagara River}}.
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amitābha}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Kṣitigarbha}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=Tenbu}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|en|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|WD=Q3071886|lt=Fujiwara no Takamitsu}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kimpusen-ji|en|Kimpusen-ji|WD=Q1741668|lt=Kinpusen-ji}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|en|Yoshino, Nara|WD=Q1203731|lt=Yoshino, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ({{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|WD=Q857243|lt=Gujo, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).

'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Mino, Gifu|en|Mino, Gifu|WD=Q853835|lt=Mino, Gifu|1=Mino, Gifu}}, Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540766|1=Six Shrines of Mount Kōga}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katachi River|1=Katachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18701005}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Itadori River|en|Itadori River|WD=Q6092348|lt=Itadori River|1=Itadori River}}, itself a tributary of the {{ill|Nagara River|en|Nagara River|WD=Q1194094|lt=Nagara River|1=Nagara River}}.
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amitābha|1=Amitābha}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Ekādaśamukha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Kṣitigarbha|1=Kṣitigarbha}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=Tenbu|1=Deva (Buddhism)}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|1=Royal court}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|en|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|WD=Q3071886|lt=Fujiwara no Takamitsu|1=Fujiwara no Takamitsu}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kimpusen-ji|en|Kimpusen-ji|WD=Q1741668|lt=Kinpusen-ji|1=Kimpusen-ji}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|en|Yoshino, Nara|WD=Q1203731|lt=Yoshino, Nara|1=Yoshino, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki, Gifu|1=Seki, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ({{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|WD=Q857243|lt=Gujo, Gifu|1=Gujō, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Mino, Gifu|en|Mino, Gifu|WD=Q853835|lt=Mino, Gifu|1=Mino, Gifu}}, Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).

Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amitābha|1=Amitābha}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Ekādaśamukha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Kṣitigarbha|1=Kṣitigarbha}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=Tenbu|1=Deva (Buddhism)}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|1=Royal court}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|en|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|WD=Q3071886|lt=Fujiwara no Takamitsu|1=Fujiwara no Takamitsu}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>

Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kimpusen-ji|en|Kimpusen-ji|WD=Q1741668|lt=Kinpusen-ji|1=Kimpusen-ji}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|en|Yoshino, Nara|WD=Q1203731|lt=Yoshino, Nara|1=Yoshino, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.

Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki, Gifu|1=Seki, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ({{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|WD=Q857243|lt=Gujo, Gifu|1=Gujō, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.

== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amitābha|1=Amitābha}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Ekādaśamukha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Kṣitigarbha|1=Kṣitigarbha}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=Tenbu|1=Deva (Buddhism)}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|1=Royal court}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|en|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|WD=Q3071886|lt=Fujiwara no Takamitsu|1=Fujiwara no Takamitsu}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kimpusen-ji|en|Kimpusen-ji|WD=Q1741668|lt=Kinpusen-ji|1=Kimpusen-ji}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|en|Yoshino, Nara|WD=Q1203731|lt=Yoshino, Nara|1=Yoshino, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki, Gifu|1=Seki, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ({{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|WD=Q857243|lt=Gujo, Gifu|1=Gujō, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Mino, Gifu|en|Mino, Gifu|WD=Q853835|lt=Mino, Gifu|1=Mino, Gifu}}, Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540766|1=Six Shrines of Mount Kōga}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katachi River|1=Katachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18701005}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Itadori River|en|Itadori River|WD=Q6092348|lt=Itadori River|1=Itadori River}}, itself a tributary of the {{ill|Nagara River|en|Nagara River|WD=Q1194094|lt=Nagara River|1=Nagara River}}.
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amitābha|1=Amitābha}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Ekādaśamukha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404731}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Kṣitigarbha|1=Kṣitigarbha}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10514059}} and {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=Tenbu|1=Deva (Buddhism)}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|1=Royal court}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|en|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|WD=Q3071886|lt=Fujiwara no Takamitsu|1=Fujiwara no Takamitsu}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kimpusen-ji|en|Kimpusen-ji|WD=Q1741668|lt=Kinpusen-ji|1=Kimpusen-ji}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|en|Yoshino, Nara|WD=Q1203731|lt=Yoshino, Nara|1=Yoshino, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki, Gifu|1=Seki, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ({{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|WD=Q857243|lt=Gujo, Gifu|1=Gujō, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.

== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672867}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki, Gifu|1=Seki, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642751}} ({{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|WD=Q857243|lt=Gujo, Gifu|1=Gujō, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/ Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association] (岐阜県神社庁)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金峰神社 (美濃市)]]
{{infobox religious building |name = Kinpō Shrine (美濃市)|native_name= {{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11488964}} | image=File:Kinbu shrine, Mino, 2013.JPG |address= {{ill|Mino, Gifu|en|Mino, Gifu|qid=Q853835|lt=Mino, Gifu|1=Mino, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|50.43|N|136|54|40.29|E|scale:20000}} |deity= {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} |創建= {{ill|Tenryaku|en|Tenryaku|qid=Q1346281|lt=Tenryaku era|1=Tenryaku}} period (947–957) |prefecture= {{ill|Mino|qid=Q853835|1=Mino, Gifu}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.5973417|N|136.9111917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Kouka jinja2009-6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Statue of "Takamitsu-kō and Sarutorahebi" at {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672867}}, {{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|qid=Q860647|lt=Seki, Gifu|1=Seki, Gifu}}]] [[File:Mount Koga from Kouka jinja (2015-01-24).JPG|right|thumb|220px|Torii gate of Kōka Shrine with {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672865}} in the background]]
'''{{ill|Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja|金峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpu Shrine (Mino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11488964}}''' (かねみねじんじゃ<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kinpu Shrine - Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association|url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2481|accessdate=2022-10-30}}</ref>) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Mino, Gifu|en|Mino, Gifu|qid=Q853835|lt=Mino, Gifu|1=Mino, Gifu}}, Japan. It is one of the six shrines that form the core of **{{ill|Mount Kōga faith|ja|高賀山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** (高賀山六社).
== Overview == The shrine is one of the **{{ill|Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山六社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Shrines of Mount Kōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135540766|1=Six Shrines of Mount Kōga}}**, located along the {{ill|Katachi River|ja|片知川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katachi River|1=Katachi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18701005}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Itadori River|en|Itadori River|qid=Q6092348|lt=Itadori River|1=Itadori River}}, itself a tributary of the {{ill|Nagara River|en|Nagara River|qid=Q1194094|lt=Nagara River|1=Nagara River}}.
Locals commonly refer to the shrine as "Kanemine Jinja" (かねみねじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}
The actual sacred objects of worship are said to be statues of: * {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amitābha|1=Amitābha}} * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|ja|十一面観世音菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekādaśamukha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十一面観音|1=Ekādaśamukha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11404731}} * {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|qid=Q604687|lt=Kṣitigarbha|1=Kṣitigarbha}}
Additionally, flanking these statues are images of {{ill|Kongō Zaō|ja|金剛蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Zaō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王権現|1=Zaō Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10514059}} and {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|qid=Q4089509|lt=Tenbu|1=Deva (Buddhism)}} deities.
== History == According to shrine tradition, during the **Tenryaku era** (947–957), the **Sarutorahebi** (さるとらへび), a mythical yokai, began terrorizing the local villages. The {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|qid=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|1=Royal court}} dispatched {{ill|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|en|Fujiwara no Takamitsu|qid=Q3071886|lt=Fujiwara no Takamitsu|1=Fujiwara no Takamitsu}} to exterminate it.<ref>Another theory suggests the event took place in **Shōhei 3** (933), but this conflicts with Fujiwara no Takamitsu's birth year.</ref>
The first sighting of the Sarutorahebi was at this location, leading to the naming of the area **"Katachi"** (形知), now known as **Katachi** (片知). A shrine was then built in commemoration of the event, originally known as **Katachi Shrine**.
Over time, {{ill|Mount Kōga|ja|高賀山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kōga|1=Mount Koga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672865}} gained reverence as a sacred site in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}. Influenced by {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|qid=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}}, the shrine was later renamed **Zaō Gongen Shrine** (蔵王権現社). During the early **Meiji period**, it was renamed **Kinpu Shrine** (金峰神社), likely in reference to {{ill|Kimpusen-ji|en|Kimpusen-ji|qid=Q1741668|lt=Kinpusen-ji|1=Kimpusen-ji}} in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|en|Yoshino, Nara|qid=Q1203731|lt=Yoshino, Nara|1=Yoshino, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.
== The Six Shrines of Mount Kōga == The **Six Shrines of Mount Kōga** are located around Mount Kōga (高賀山), {{ill|Mount Hyōga|ja|瓢ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hyōga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mount Imabuchi|ja|今渕ケ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Imabuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mount Katachi|ja|片知山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Katachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Traditionally, ascetics would complete a **one-day pilgrimage** known as **Six Shrine Pilgrimage** (六社めぐり), trekking across mountain ridges.
The six shrines are: * {{ill|Kōka Shrine (Seki)|ja|高賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōka Shrine (Seki)|1=Kōka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672867}} ({{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|qid=Q860647|lt=Seki, Gifu|1=Seki, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Shingū Shrine|ja|那比新宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Shingū Shrine|1=Nabi Shingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11642751}} ({{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|qid=Q857243|lt=Gujo, Gifu|1=Gujō, Gifu}}) * {{ill|Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja|本宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabi Hongū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 那比本宮神社|1=Nabi Hongū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28684739}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Hoshimiya Shrine|ja|星宮神社 (郡上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshimiya Shrine|qq=}} (Gujo, Gifu) * {{ill|Taki Shrine (Mino)|ja|瀧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine (Mino)|1=Taki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11566355}} (Mino, Gifu) * **Kinpu Shrine** (Mino, Gifu)
== Access == * **By Bus:** {{ill|Gifu Bus|en|Gifu Bus|qid=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} **Makidani Line** – Get off at **Kanemonbashi (金門橋) Bus Stop**.
